chapter_title.en: Chapter on the Inheritance of the Child of Adultery

text: 5681 - رَوَى اَلْحُسَيْنُ بْنُ سَعِيدٍ عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ اَلْحَسَنِ بْنِ أَبِي خَالِدٍ اَلْأَشْعَرِيِّ قَالَ : كَتَبَ بَعْضُ أَصْحَابِنَا إِلَى أَبِي جَعْفَرٍ اَلثَّانِي عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ مَعِي يَسْأَلُهُ عَنْ رَجُلٍ فَجَرَ بِامْرَأَةٍ فَحَمَلَتْ ثُمَّ إِنَّهُ تَزَوَّجَهَا بَعْدَ اَلْحَمْلِ فَجَاءَتْ بِوَلَدٍ وَ اَلْوَلَدُ أَشْبَهُ خَلْقِ اَللَّهِ بِهِ فَكَتَبَ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ بِخَطِّهِ وَ خَاتَمِهِ "اَلْوَلَدُ لِغَيَّةٍ لاَ يُورَثُ".

en.bab-ul-qaim-publications:
Hadith.5681 - Al-Husayn ibn Sa'id narrated from Muhammad ibn Al-Hasan ibn Abi Khalid Al-Ash'ari, who said: One of our companions wrote to Abu Ja'far Al-Thani (as) through me, asking Imam (as) about a man who committed adultery with a woman, and she became pregnant. Later, he married her after the pregnancy, and she gave birth to a child who resembled him more than anyone else in creation.
Imam (as) wrote back in his own handwriting and with his seal:
"The child is the result of fornication and does not inherit."
